task:
I started a new session. We are now working on @specs/phases/03-export-zip-and-github-branch-fork.md . I asked the agent to interview me about this, and it did, but it didn’t properly number things. so I was afraid of context getting too bloated. Why don’t you reframe these questions back to me with proper numbering:

Perfect — let’s run this like a real system design interview for Phase 3.

I’ll skip obvious stuff and only ask decisions that meaningfully change implementation/risk.
(Defaults I’m already assuming unless you override: GitHub App auth, no force-push, 7d retention, 15m signed URLs.)


Answer in short bullets with the question number.


Fork commit model
Should fork create one squashed commit representing workspace delta, or preserve commit structure somehow?
(My strong default: one deterministic squashed commit per fork operation for auditability + simplicity.)
Baseline mismatch guard
If target repo default branch has moved since workspace baseline commit, do we:
A) still fork from original baseline SHA (possibly stale), or
B) require branch-from-current-head with rebase/merge attempt, or
C) fail requiring user to refresh workspace?
(Default: A for determinism, with response warning baseline_stale=true.)
Diff application strategy
When applying workspace changes to target branch, should we:
A) materialize full workspace tree and commit snapshot, or
B) apply generated patch to baseline checkout?
(Default: A for reliability; B only as fallback for patch export path.)
Cross-repo safety policy
Can users fork to any repo the App can access, or only same owner/org as source?
(Default: org allowlist + explicit source→target policy table to prevent accidental exfiltration.)
Binary and large-file handling
For zip/patch/fork, what’s policy when workspace includes binaries or files exceeding threshold (e.g., >25MB each)?
include in zip always?
exclude from patch?
block fork if over GitHub blob limits?
(Default: zip includes all; patch excludes binaries with manife

task:
I’ll just got with all your commendations here. Update the @specs/phases/03-export-zip-and-github-branch-fork.md file with these decisions
Called the Read tool with the following input: {"filePath":"/Users/nickdejesus/Code/nimbus/specs/phases/03-export-zip-and-github-branch-fork.md"}
<path>/Users/nickdejesus/Code/nimbus/specs/phases/03-export-zip-and-github-branch-fork.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: # Phase 3: Export Zip and GitHub Branch Fork
2: 
3: ## Objective
4: Let users take workspace changes out of Nimbus safely via downloadable artifacts or GitHub branch forks.
5: 
6: ## Decision
7: - Git provider support in v1: GitHub only.
8: - GitHub auth mode in v1: GitHub App installation tokens only (no stored user PAT/OAuth tokens).
9: 
10: ## In scope
11: - Export workspace snapshot as zip artifact.
12: - Optional patch export (`.patch`) from workspace diff.
13: - Fork workspace changes into a GitHub branch.
14: - Persist export/fork events and artifact metadata.
15: 
16: ## Out of scope
17: - Non-GitHub SCM integrations.
18: - Auto-merge workflows.
19: - PR review automation (can be follow-up).
20: 
21: ## User stories
22: 1. As a developer, I can download current workspace state as zip.
23: 2. As a developer, I can create a GitHub branch with workspace changes.
24: 3. As a developer, I can see whether export/fork succeeded and retrieve outputs.
25: 
26: ## Deliverables
27: - D1 schema additions:
28:   - `workspace_artifacts`
29: - Worker APIs:
30:   - `POST /api/workspaces/:id/export/zip`
31:   - `POST /api/workspaces/:id/export/patch`
32:   - `POST /api/workspaces/:id/fork/github`
33:   - `GET /api/workspaces/:id/artifacts`
34: - Artifact storage:
35:   - Store zip/patch in R2 with retention metadata.
36:   - Signed URL generation with short TTL.
37: - GitHub fork flow:
38:   - Create branch from source commit SHA.
39:   - Apply workspace diff.
40:   - Commit and push to target repo/branch.
41: 
42: ## API contract notes
43: - Fork request should include:

  -F "metadata=$(cat /tmp/nimbus-metadata.json)” \
  -F "bundle=@/tmp/nimbus-export-demo.tar.gz;type=application/gzip” \

unzip -l /tmp/nimbus-export.zip

okay, I want to give you more automony here. I want you to review the @specs/phases/04-agentic-runtime.md Spec. Determine what needs to be done. If you have any questions that would help flesh out the details, think about them but you should be the one that answers them and use what you would recommend to me. There are a few I want to answer tho:

Agent autonomy level should be full
Model/provider should be switchable with the CloudFlare agent SDK, make sure you add that documentation in there.
Retry policy I’ll let you try.

You handle the rest of the questions you’d ask me with your recommendations.

Create a new branch (don’t forget to do this part).

Build out the whole thing, test everything as you build. When you’re done and feature complete, I want you to then run the review command. If the review command fins a bug, fix it. Then run review again. Repeat this loop until the reviewer doesn’t find any more bugs. When you hit that point, stop and wait for me. Don’t make any commits.
Called the Read tool with the following input: {"filePath":"/Users/nickdejesus/Code/nimbus/specs/phases/04-agentic-runtime.md"}
<path>/Users/nickdejesus/Code/nimbus/specs/phases/04-agentic-runtime.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: # Phase 4: Agentic Runtime
2: 
3: ## Objective
4: Enable autonomous or guided agent tasks to modify workspace code safely and observably.
5: 
6: ## In scope
7: - Agent task model and queue/workflow execution.
8: - Tool surface for agent actions against workspace.
9: - Streaming task events and durable task logs.
10: - Guardrails for command/file operations.
11: 
12: ## Out of scope
13: - Full generalized multi-agent marketplace.
14: - Unbounded shell access.
15: - Human approval UX beyond minimal endpoint hooks.
16: 
17: ## User stories
18: 1. As a developer, I can ask an agent to apply changes in my workspace.
19: 2. As a developer, I can watch progress and inspect outputs/errors.
20: 3. As a developer, I can review resulting diff before export/fork.
Summarize the task tool output above and continue with your task.
